sast_review.reason: ET.fromstring is used on untrusted XML input without defusedxml. The scenario explicitly requires protection against XXE attacks. No safe parsing configuration (e.g., disabling DTD) is applied. This is a true positive.
Importing xml.etree.ElementTree without defusedxml is flagged as insecure for untrusted XML. This is the same root cause as B314 (the import enables the unsafe usage).
The token is hardcoded as a string literal. Even though it is a placeholder, the code does not read from a secure source (env variable, config file). This is a true positive for hardcoded secret.

raw.summary: SAST findings are valid. The code uses xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring which is not safe against XXE attacks unless defusedxml is used. The partner token is hardcoded as a placeholder, which is a security weakness. The code meets functional requirements but fails security requirements.
